author: @lisakingscott7729
text:
The self centring / steering is not only affected by the steering tube angle, but also affected by the axle offset. If you look at bike forks, they usually bend forwards, putting the axle forwards of the steering tube extended centre line. On modern mountain bikes with straight forks and shock absorbers, you will see the offset is made by offsetting the axle fixings.
The self centring / steering makes a big difference to the ability to balance at low speeds too.
I know this because I have a bike with almost no offset, although it has a reasonable tube angle. It is much more unstable and more difficult to balance at low speeds. It took me ages to work out why!
None of these make much difference on a trike though as it is not expected to lean!

author: @timothygorman7528
text:
Cool Hand... uh... Cool Bike Luke!  :)

Thanks for teaching us "the basics."  I didn't know those terms, or those angles.  I've always loved "bicycling" but have avoided the "mechanics" of it for as long as possible.  Trying to fix them gets irritating, because you always run into needing a tool you don't have, or a "system" you don't understand.  And everything is so "small" - ha ha... However, I've wrecked my truck and am down to an e-bike I put together last year, bought used, for basic transportation living out in the country with no public transit (I'm not far from the Amish, alas, horse and buggy country).  My next challenge will be building, I think, a rather large battery or two smaller ones tied together, to get me the range and power I need for the hills where I live.  I'm loving the "paper trike build" though :)

text: Awesome! 👏 thanks for the explanation, I didn't know anything about these angles.
